title: Berrya mariarum
intro: Berrya mariarum, also known as the "Kerala berry," is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing inflammation, protecting against oxidative stress, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Berrya mariarum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
